w:r.w:t: be referred to as Cetirizine Tablets throughout this leaflet.
not listed in this leaflet. See section 4.
What you need to know before you take Cetirizine
Contents of the pack and other
1. WHAT CETIRIZINE TABLETS ARE AND WHAT THEY ARE USED FOR
Tell your doctor if you are pregnant, think you might be pregnant, or are planning to become pregnant.
Cetirizine Tablets should be avoided in pregnant women.
You should not take Cetirizine Tablets during
Clinical studies have produced no evidence of impaired attention, alertness and driving capabilities

potentially hazardous activities or operate machinery, you should not exceed the recommended dose. You should closely observe your response to the drug. Do not drive or operate machinery if this product makes you drowsy.
; if you have been told by your doctor that you have an intolerance
your doctor before taking these tablets.
The active ingredient in your tablets is cetirizine hydrochloride, which belongs to a group of medicines called antihistamines. These block the effects of a substance called histamine which occurs
involved in allergic reactions. Cetirizine is used to relieve the

hay fever), perennial allergic rhinitis (e.g. year round allergies often due to house dust mites or animal allergies) and urticaria (itchy, red, swollen skin). These symptoms include itchy skin rashes; sneezing; itchy, runny or blocked nose; red, itchy and watering eyes.
2. WHAT YOU NEED TO KNOW BEFORE YOU TAKE CETIRIZINE TABLETS
to cetirizine hydrochloride, hydroxyzine, piperazine derivatives or any of the
(severe renal failure with creatine clearance below
If you are a patient with liver problems or renal insufficiency, please ask your doctor for advice; if
new dose will be determined by your doctor.
If you have spinal cord lesion or enlargement of the
Please tell your doctor if you are taking or have recently
as they may decrease or increase the effect of Cetirizine Tablets and vice versa.
It may still be alright for you to take Cetirizine Tablets and your doctor will be able to decide what
Tablets can affect the results of allergy skin tests. If you require an allergy test, you should
Tablets with food, drink and alcohol
Cetirizine Tablets can be taken with or without food.

while using Cetirizine. It can increase the effects of alcohol. No interactions susceptible to have a noticeable impact have been observed between alcohol (at the blood level of 0.5 per ml corresponding to one glass of wine) and cetirizine used at the normal doses. However, as is the case with
as your doctor or pharmacist has told you to do so. You
pharmacist if you are not sure
5 mg twice daily (a half tablet twice
Adults and children over 12 years and over:
Take one tablet (10 mg) once daily. If the tablets make
tablet twice a day may be better than taking one tablet once a day. Cetirizine tablets are not suitable for children under 6.
Patients with moderate kidney problems are recommended
have to be adjusted on an individual basis taking into account the renal clearance, age and weight.
If you have accidentally taken more than the recommended
hospital casualty department or tell your doctor or pharmacist
. Remember to take the pack and any remaining tablets with you.
The most common signs and symptoms of overdose may include feeling unwell, confused, restless, shaky, dizzy, tired or very sleepy. You may also notice a rapid heartbeat, having dilated pupils
headache, itching, diarrhoea, or difficulty urinating.
be referred to as Cetirizine Tablets throughout this leaflet.
not listed in this leaflet. See section 4.

1. WHAT CETIRIZINE TABLETS ARE AND WHAT THEY ARE USED FOR
Tell your doctor if you are pregnant, think you might be pregnant, or are planning to become pregnant.
Cetirizine Tablets should be avoided in pregnant women.
You should not take Cetirizine Tablets during
Clinical studies have produced no evidence of impaired attention, alertness and driving capabilities

potentially hazardous activities or operate machinery, you should not exceed the recommended dose. You should closely observe your response to the drug. Do not drive or operate machinery if this product makes you drowsy.

The active ingredient in your tablets is cetirizine hydrochloride, which belongs to a group of medicines called antihistamines. These block the effects of a substance called histamine which occurs
involved in allergic reactions. Cetirizine is used to relieve the

hay fever), perennial allergic rhinitis (e.g. year round allergies often due to house dust mites or animal allergies) and urticaria (itchy, red, swollen skin). These symptoms include itchy skin rashes; sneezing; itchy, runny or blocked nose; red, itchy and watering eyes.

5 mg twice daily (a half tablet twice

hospital casualty department or tell your doctor or pharmacist
. Remember to take the pack and any remaining tablets with you.
The most common signs and symptoms of overdose may include feeling unwell, confused, restless, shaky, dizzy, tired or very sleepy. You may also notice a rapid heartbeat, having dilated pupils
headache, itching, diarrhoea, or difficulty urinating.
as soon as you remember, but wait at least 24 hours before taking your next dose.
of this product, ask your doctor or pharmacist.
that adequate oral hygiene is maintained
you notice any side eﬀects not listed in this leaflet, please tell your doctor or pharmacist.
If you get any side effects, talk to your doctor or pharmacist.
effects not listed in this leaflet. You can also report side effects directly via the Yellow Card Scheme,

help provide more information on the safety of this
Like all medicines, Cetirizine Tablets can cause side
If you get any of the following symptoms after taking these tablets you
should contact your doctor or pharmacist immediately:
a severe allergic reaction including rash, itchiness
face, lips or throat. This reaction may start soon after you
In very rare cases people have thought about committing
then stop taking the tablets and see your doctor.
Common side eﬀects (may aﬀect up to 1 in 10
of the lining of the nose
the possibility of damage to the liver.
blurred vision, difficulty in focusing the eyes, having
swelling of the face, lips or
muscle spasms in the neck and
and numerous small, superficial pustules, arising within large areas of redness
on the carton. The expiry date refers to the last day of that month. This medicinal product does not require any special storage conditions.
Medicines should not be disposed of via waste water or household waste. Ask your pharmacist how to dispose of medicines no longer required. These measures will help to protect the environment.
6. CONTENTS OF THE PACK AND OTHER INFORMATION
dihydrochloride. Each tablet contains 10 mg cetirizine
The other ingredients are: lactose monohydrate, microcrystalline cellulose, colloidal anhydrous silica, maize starch, talc, magnesium stearate, titanium
marked with “A” on one side, and a break-line on the
7, 14 and 30 tablets. Not all pack sizes may be

formats upon request. This leaflet was last revised in September 2025.
